text.title: Agent Integration for Adobe Firefly
text.summary: Agentic workflow, API auth pattern, service catalog, and gotchas for automating Firefly asset generation.

rule.statement: Agents drive Firefly via Firefly Services REST API only; in-Figma plugin usage requires a human at the keyboard.
rule.rationale: Firefly cannot be driven headlessly from inside Figma. The Services API (enterprise) is the only agent-compatible surface for batch operations.

rule.statement: Implement OAuth2 client-credentials token refresh; Firefly Services tokens expire every 24 hours.
rule.rationale: A batch pipeline that forgets token renewal will fail silently mid-run. Proactive refresh before expiry is required.

rule.statement: Use numVariations: 3 and require a human to select; never auto-select generated images for production.
rule.rationale: AI aesthetic outputs are non-deterministic and brand-inconsistent at scale. Human selection is the quality gate.

list.item: Adobe Firefly Services (enterprise SaaS) — REST API, OAuth2 machine-to-machine, requires enterprise plan
Photoshop API (enterprise SaaS) — headless PS: remove-bg, smart-crop, generative fill
Cloudinary (SaaS) — CDN and transformation layer for Firefly-generated assets
Bynder / Brandfolder (SaaS DAM) — approved asset destination; enforces brand consistency
imagemagick — post-process downloaded assets (resize, crop, convert)
rclone — sync generated assets to S3/GCS/Cloudflare R2

antipattern.reason: Firefly API rate limits (typically 10 req/s) require exponential backoff. Latency per generation is 2–8s; synchronous tight loops will exhaust limits and fail.
Credit consumption is opaque for automated pipelines; large batches exhaust allotments quickly. Monitor per-project with a dashboard and alert before budget exhaustion.

text.reference: Batch generation script: CSV of prompts → Firefly API → downloaded assets
